title: rosa gallica var. atropurpurea
intro: Rosa gallica var. atropurpurea, also known as the purple gallica rose, is a variety of the historic Rosa gallica rose species, characterized by its dark purple, fragrant flowers. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally employed to treat a range of health conditions. The medicinal properties of Rosa gallica var. atropurpurea have been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Traditionally, the plant's flowers and leaves are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
